summary: Installer filter feature testing completed successfully. All UI elements work correctly: dropdown loads installers from API, filter selection shows badge, combined filters work, and 'Limpar todos' clears filters properly.
critical_code_review_comments: Installer filter implementation is correct - uses installer.id for filtering
The filter badge correctly shows 'Instalador: [name]' with purple styling
Filter logic properly checks job.assigned_installers array

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All installer filter UI features working

context_for_next_testing_agent: Installer filter on Jobs page is fully functional. Features tested: (1) Dropdown loads 17 installers from /api/installers API, (2) Selecting installer shows purple badge 'Instalador: [name]', (3) Filter uses installer.id to match job.assigned_installers array, (4) Combined filters work correctly (installer + status shown as separate badges), (5) 'Limpar todos' button clears all filters, (6) Individual filter X button removes specific filter. Note: Preview environment has 2 jobs (both finalized), so job list appears empty with default filters (finalized jobs are hidden by design).

features_tested.installer_dropdown: PASS - Loads 17 installers from API (18 options including 'Todos os Instaladores')
features_tested.installer_filter_badge: PASS - Shows purple badge 'Instalador: [name]' when filter active
features_tested.combined_filters: PASS - Status and Installer badges display together correctly
features_tested.individual_filter_removal: PASS - X button next to badge removes specific filter
features_tested.filter_logic: PASS - Code correctly checks if installerFilter is in job.assigned_installers array

api_endpoints_tested.GET /api/installers: 200 OK - Returns 17 installers with id, full_name, branch, etc.

notes: The Jobs page shows 0 jobs because both existing jobs (Job Teste Completo - Checkin Flow and Job Teste Agendamento) have status 'finalizado', which is hidden by default filter. This is expected behavior per the code logic.
